title: Seed the enforcement rule
evidence: The Testing and Enforcement section declares a domain-to-infrastructure forbidden rule without seededViolation metadata.
finding: The enforcement claim can pass vacuously because the design does not name a seed or standing command that proves the rule fails.
proposed_fix: Add seededViolation metadata to the forbidden rule and cite the architecture gate that proves the seed fails.
rationale: Negative architecture claims rot unless the gate is proven against an intentional violation.
gate_ref: DDD review rubric: An enforcement rule has no seeded violation or standing gate.

title: Source both invariant operands
evidence: The invariant says approved amount matches order, but Source operands names only approvedAmount.
finding: The design omits the source for the order amount operand, so implementers cannot tell where the comparison value is owned.
proposed_fix: Name both operands as declared fields, events, projections, or resolver outputs.
rationale: Relational predicates are not checkable unless each operand has a source and owner.
gate_ref: DDD review rubric: A relational predicate names only one operand or uses a category instead of declared fields/events.

title: Name the public port surface
evidence: The design says PaymentGatewayPort is public without naming an export path, import surface, or proof.
finding: Consumers may import an internal module path because the public exposure surface is only promised in prose.
proposed_fix: Name the public export or package surface and add an import proof or manual exposure checklist.
rationale: A public API is not durable unless the design names how downstream code reaches it.
gate_ref: DDD review rubric: A public API is promised in prose but no public export/import surface or import test is named.

title: Make the planner handoff extractable
evidence: The Planner Handoff Summary only says the design is ready for planning and points to sections below, without stable source, boundary, delivery, validation, or stop-condition IDs.
finding: Planning would have to infer required handoff facts from DDD prose, so the design can pass as planning-ready without a real contract surface.
proposed_fix: Add a Planner Handoff Summary with stable source, boundary, delivery, validation, and stop-condition IDs.
rationale: Planning consumes the methodology-neutral handoff contract, not DDD-specific authoring sections.
gate_ref: DDD review rubric: A design marks ready for planning without a `Planner Handoff Summary` that names stable source, boundary, delivery, validation, and stop-condition IDs.
